{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == {{small|(References used in article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2019|chapter=|title=Tobiozuka Kofun I - Mound Survey and Stone Chamber Measurement Report|publisher={{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} Graduate School of Social and Cultural Sciences Archaeology Laboratory|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used in article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2023|chapter=|title=Tobiozuka Kofun II - Stone Chamber and Mound Excavation Overview|publisher=Okayama University Graduate School of Social and Cultural Sciences Archaeology Laboratory|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|鳶尾塚古墳II|2023}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author1=Shunta Kaneko|author2=Toshio Nosaka|author3=Akira Seike|title=Petrological Characteristics of Stone Chamber Materials of Tobiozuka Kofun|date=2024-03-31|publisher={{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} Faculty of Science, Department of Earth Science|journal=Okayama University Earth Science Reports|volume=30|number=1|pages=1-12|link=https://ousar.lib.okayama-u.ac.jp/files/public/6/66844/20240402085749352251/esr_030_001_012.pdf|ref=}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tobiozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}

This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "造山古墳}}" to "造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == {{small|(References used in article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2019|chapter=|title=Tobiozuka Kofun I - Mound Survey and Stone Chamber Measurement Report|publisher={{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} Graduate School of Social and Cultural Sciences Archaeology Laboratory|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Additional literature not used in article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2023|chapter=|title=Tobiozuka Kofun II - Stone Chamber and Mound Excavation Overview|publisher=Okayama University Graduate School of Social and Cultural Sciences Archaeology Laboratory|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|鳶尾塚古墳II|2023}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author1=Shunta Kaneko|author2=Toshio Nosaka|author3=Akira Seike|title=Petrological Characteristics of Stone Chamber Materials of Tobiozuka Kofun|date=2024-03-31|publisher={{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} Faculty of Science, Department of Earth Science|journal=Okayama University Earth Science Reports|volume=30|number=1|pages=1-12|link=https://ousar.lib.okayama-u.ac.jp/files/public/6/66844/20240402085749352251/esr_030_001_012.pdf|ref=}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tobiozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large burial mounds in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
