'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial_facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} |associated_burials = |construction_period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |Burial Facility = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_burials = |built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_burials = |built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 22.95 |longitude_degrees =133|longitude_minutes = 47|longitude_seconds = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_burials = |built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |icon = Round tumulus |label = Tobiozuka Kofun |label_position = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. Artifacts such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designated as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
