'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinatesonds = 22.95 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 47|coordinates = 10.95 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_burials = |built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tobiozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinatesonds = 22.95 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 47|coordinates = 10.95 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_burials = |built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_notes = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tobiozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * Burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternative_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinatesonds = 22.95 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 47|coordinates = 10.95 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_burials = |built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_property = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tobiozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not other_designation1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year }} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市|12=draft}} Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinatesonds = 22.95 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 47|coordinates = 10.95 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |associated_burials = |built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_notes = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tobiozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=draft}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year|12=draft}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学|12=draft}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=draft}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|12=draft}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=draft}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|ja|2017th year|12=draft}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|ja|岡山大学|12=draft}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=draft}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|12=draft}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmoritsuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=draft}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|12=draft}}.
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鳶尾塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション =墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[総社市]]下林539-2・540（字鳶尾） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 22.95 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 10.95 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径23m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Okayama |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鳶尾塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''鳶尾塚古墳'''（とびおづかこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[総社市]]下林にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。
== 概要 == 岡山県南部、三須丘陵の山頂から北東方向に延びる尾根上に築造された古墳である。[[2017年]]度（[[平成]]29年度）以降に[[岡山大学]]考古学研究室による墳丘測量・石室実測調査および墳丘・石室発掘調査が実施されている。
墳形は円形で、直径約23メートルを測る{{Sfn|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}。墳丘は2段築成以上{{Sfn|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、東方向に開口する。石室全長約12.5メートルを測る大型石室で、特に玄室奥側の天井石に長さ5メートル以上・幅2メートル以上の巨石を使用する点で特色を示す。副葬品は詳らかでないが、石室内の実測調査では[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片が採集されている。
築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期-[[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]前半（TK209型式期以降）頃と推定される{{Sfn|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}。一帯では[[こうもり塚古墳]]・[[江崎古墳]]に後続する時期に位置づけられる可能性が高く、[[造山古墳]]・[[作山古墳]]以来の備中南部地域における大型古墳群としては最後の古墳になる可能性があるとして、古墳時代の終焉を考察するうえで注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}。
== 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築され、東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}。 * 石室全長：約12.5メートル * 玄室：長さ約6.4メートル（南壁）・約7.5メートル（北壁）、幅約2.3メートル、現在高さ約2.3メートル * 羨道：長さ約6メートル（南壁）・約5メートル（北壁）、幅約1.6メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室・羨道の袖部は左右対称ではなく、南壁（右壁）の袖部は玄室寄り、北壁（左壁）の袖部は開口部寄りにあり、北壁の袖部の突出は小さく玄室と羨道の区別が不明瞭である。玄室の奥壁は1枚石、両側壁は2-3段積みで、石材間には粘質土を充填する。天井石は2枚。特に奥壁側の天井石は、長さ5メートル以上・幅2メートル以上を測る非常に大型の石材になる。羨道は、基本的に2-3段積みで、同様に粘質土を充填する。天井石は3枚{{Sfn|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}。
袖部を左右非対称とする石室は瀬戸内・四国地方の角塚型石室で認められており、これらの地域との交流が指摘される{{Sfn|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}。 <gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2019|chapter=|title=鳶尾塚古墳I -墳丘測量調査・石室実測調査報告-|publisher=[[岡山大学]]大学院社会文化科学研究科考古学研究室|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|鳶尾塚古墳I|2019}}}}
== 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2023|chapter=|title=鳶尾塚古墳II -石室・墳丘発掘調査概報-|publisher=岡山大学大学院社会文化科学研究科考古学研究室|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|鳶尾塚古墳II|2023}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author1=金子峻大|author2=野坂俊夫|author3=清家章|title=鳶尾塚古墳の石室石材の岩石学的特徴|date=2024-03-31|publisher=[[岡山大学]]理学部地球科学科|journal=Okayama University Earth Science Reports（岡山大学地球科学研究報告）|volume=30|number=1|pages=1-12|link=https://ousar.lib.okayama-u.ac.jp/files/public/6/66844/20240402085749352251/esr_030_001_012.pdf|ref=}}
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tobiozuka Kofun}} * [[こうもり塚古墳]] * [[江崎古墳]]
{{デフォルトソート:とひおつかこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]]

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.

== Overview ==

The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.

The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.

The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].

== burial ==

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]]
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows:
* Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters
* burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters
* Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

</gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.

== Overview ==

The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.

The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.

The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].

== burial ==

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]]
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows:
* Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters
* burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters
* Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.

== Overview ==

The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.

The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.

The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].

== burial ==

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]]
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows:
* Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters
* burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters
* Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
