This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.

The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].

== burial ==

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]]
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows:
* Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters
* burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters
* Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.

The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.

The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). == burial ==

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]]
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows:
* Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters
* burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.

== Overview ==

The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.

The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.

The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].

== burial ==

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]]
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows:
* Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters
* burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters
* Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.

== Overview ==

The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.

The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.

The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].

== burial ==

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]]
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows:
* Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters
* burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters
* Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinates = 22.95 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 47|coordinates = 10.95 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_burials = |built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_notes = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tobiozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. == burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinates = 22.95 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 47|coordinates = 10.95 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_burials = |built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_notes = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tobiozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinates = 22.95 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 47|coordinates = 10.95 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_burials = |built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_notes = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tobiozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinates = 22.95 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 47|coordinates = 10.95 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_burials = |built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_notes = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tobiozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tobiozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] Shimobayashi 539-2, 540 (Tobio) |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinates = 22.95 |coordinatesrees =133|coordinates = 47|coordinates = 10.95 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 23 m |burial = Both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_burials = |built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historical_site = None |tangible_notes = |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tobiozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview ==
The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since [[:en:2017|2017]] (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of [[:en:Okayama University|Okayama University]] has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been collected during chamber measurements.
The construction period is estimated to be from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the [[:en:Kōmorizuka Kofun|Kōmorizuka Kofun]] and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]].
== burial ==
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]
The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows: * Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters * burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters * Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters
The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.
The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.
<gallery> Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance) Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber) Tobiozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.

== Overview ==

The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|en|2017|WD=Q25290|lt=2017}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|en|Okayama University|WD=Q1067916|lt=Okayama University}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.

The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.

The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|en|Kōmorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11266204|lt=Kōmorizuka Kofun}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|WD=Q11639745|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|WD=Q11385355|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}}.

== burial ==

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]]
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows:
* Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters
* burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters
* Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

'''{{ill|Tobiotsuka Kofun|ja|鳶尾塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobiotsuka Kofun|qq=}}''' (鳶尾塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a round tumulus, not Historic Site1 as a historic site.

== Overview ==

The Tobiozuka Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending northeast from the summit of the Mitsu Hills in southern Okayama Prefecture. Since {{ill|2017|en|2017|WD=Q25290|lt=2017}} (Heisei 29), the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|en|Okayama University|WD=Q1067916|lt=Okayama University}} has conducted mound surveys, stone chamber measurements, and excavation surveys of the tumulus and chamber.

The mound is circular, measuring approximately 23 meters in diameter. The mound is built in at least two tiers. The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the east. This large stone chamber is about 12.5 meters in total length and features a unique ceiling stone in the rear of the chamber, measuring over 5 meters in length and 2 meters in width. burial goods such as fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} have been collected during chamber measurements.

The construction period is estimated to be from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} to its terminal phase, around the late 6th to early 7th century (after the TK209 style period). It is thought to be one of the last large Kofuns in southern Bitchū following the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|en|Kōmorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11266204|lt=Kōmorizuka Kofun}} and {{ill|Ezaki Kofun|ja|江崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezaki Kofun|qq=}}, marking the conclusion of the era of large kofun groups in the region since the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|WD=Q11639745|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|en|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|WD=Q11385355|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}}.

== burial ==

[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]]
[[File:Tobiozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]]

The burial facility is a both-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the east. The chamber measurements are as follows:
* Total chamber length: approximately 12.5 meters
* burial chamber: Length approximately 6.4 meters (south wall), 7.5 meters (north wall); width approximately 2.3 meters; current height approximately 2.3 meters
* Entrance passage: Length approximately 6 meters (south wall), 5 meters (north wall); width approximately 1.6 meters; current height approximately 1.8 meters

The burial chamber and entrance passage have asymmetrical side sections. The southern (right) side section is closer to the burial chamber, while the northern (left) side section is closer to the entrance. The distinction between the chamber and passage is unclear on the northern side due to minimal protrusion. The rear wall of the chamber consists of a single stone, and the side walls are stacked in 2–3 tiers, filled with clay. The ceiling stone consists of two slabs, with the rear stone being notably large (over 5 meters long and 2 meters wide). The entrance passage is also built with 2–3 tiers of stacked stones and clay filling, and its ceiling is composed of three stones.

The asymmetrical side sections suggest connections with corner-chambered styles found in the Setouchi and Shikoku regions, indicating cultural exchanges.

Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward rear wall)
Tobiozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward entrance)
Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward entrance)
Tobiozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage (view toward burial chamber)

</gallery>
